title: The World’s Data Lifelines Are Increasingly Exposed to Sabotage
summary: Undersea cables are vital for delivering renewable power from offshore projects and providing cross-country telecommunications connectivity. title: Dangote at Full Throttle as Nigeria Becomes a Net Fuel Exporter
summary: Nigeria is finally starting to fix a long-standing imbalance: a major crude exporter that couldn’t meet its own fuel demand. That shift is now materializing in hard numbers—and it is being driven entirely by the Dangote refinery, which is already running close to full capacity and shows little sign…

title: A New Solar Breakthrough Could Make Wood Part of the Energy Grid
summary: As the world adds solar and wind energy capacity at an unprecedented scale, we are increasingly faced with an energy variability crisis. title: Aluminum Market Faces Largest Supply Shock Since 2000, Mercuria Warns

title: US Oil Drillers Scale Back as Global Supply Crunch Continues
summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 544, down 43 from this same time last year. title: The golden age of arbitrage has begun
summary: The law of one price is in retreat — with major consequences for profits, inflation and innovation
